zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Comyn於1248年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵時，她的父親Sir Alexander Comyn 6th Earl of Buchan, Constable and Justiciar of Scotland31歲，母親Elizabeth de Quincy28歲。她和Sir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angus至少有1個兒子。她去世於1328年2月17日在英國諾圖波藍德普盧多享年80歲，埋葬在英國諾圖波藍德可哈姆聖 安德也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Comyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cumming
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): most probably a habitational name from Bosc-Benard-Commin in Eure, France. This place lies on the edge of the Forêt de Rouvray, near Rouen, in the heart of the district from which came numerous Domesday barons, and so is much more likely to be the source of the name than Comines in Nord. The name is also possibly derived from the personal name Cumin, which is found in Norfolk, Lincolnshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th and 13th centuries and may be of Breton origin, according to Reaney. Compare Old Breton Cunmin, which also appears as Cumin, and is from con- ‘hound’ with an uncertain second element, perhaps the min ‘edge’ which is found in other personal names.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuimín (or Ó Cuimín) ‘son (or ‘descendant’) of Cuimín’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of cam ‘crooked’. There was a 7th-century abbot of Iona named Cumin. In Scotland, this Irish surname became confused with the Norman name.

History: A notable bearer of the Norman surname is Wilhelmus Comyn, Bishop of Durham, who came north to Scotland with King David I in 1124 and was appointed Chancellor of Scotland. He established his nephew Richard in Tynedale and Roxburghshire, and Richard also rose to be Chancellor of Scotland. The Comyn or Cumming family gained the earldoms of Buchan and Menteith and rose to be the most powerful noble family in Scotland in the century before the rise of King Robert Bruce.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Comyn was born in 1248, in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Alexander Comyn 6th Earl of Buchan, Constable and Justiciar of Scotland, was 31 and her mother, Elizabeth de Quincy, was 28. She had at least 1 son with Sir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angus. She died on 17 February 1328, in Prudhoe, Northumberland, England, at the age of 80, and was buried in St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Comyn est née en 1248, à Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Alexander Comyn 6th Earl of Buchan, Constable and Justiciar of Scotland, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Quincy, avait 28 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angus. Elle est décédée le 17 février 1328, à Prudhoe, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterrée à St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberland, Angleterre.

de.St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberla: St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberland, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Comyn 1248 in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Alexander Comyn 6th Earl of Buchan, Constable and Justiciar of Scotland 31 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de Quincy 28. Sie hatte mit Sir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angus mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 17. Februar 1328 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Prudhoe, Northumberland, England und wurde in St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberland, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Comyn è nata il nel 1248 nel Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Alexander Comyn 6th Earl of Buchan, Constable and Justiciar of Scotland, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth de Quincy, aveva 28 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angus. Morì il 17 febbraio 1328, nel Prudhoe, Northumberland, England, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolta a St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberland, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Comynが1248年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアブチャンで生まれた時，父Sir Alexander Comyn 6th Earl of Buchan, Constable and Justiciar of Scotlandは31歳，母Elizabeth de Quincyは28歳であった。彼女はSir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angusとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1328年2月17日にイギリスノースウンバーランドプルドhオで80歳で死去し，イギリスノースウンバーランドヘクスハムスト アンドリュー'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberla: 영국 노르스벌랜드 헤스함 스트 앤드루'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Comyn(이)가 1248년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 버챈에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Alexander Comyn 6th Earl of Buchan, Constable and Justiciar of Scotland(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Quincy(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 Sir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angus(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1328년 2월 17일 영국 노르스벌랜드 플루드호에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르스벌랜드 헤스함 스트 앤드루'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Comyn nasceu em 1248, em Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Alexander Comyn 6th Earl of Buchan, Constable and Justiciar of Scotland, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Quincy, tinha 28 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angus. Ela faleceu em 17 de fevereiro de 1328, em Prudhoe, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 80 anos, e foi sepultada em St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberland, Inglaterra.

ru.St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberla: ст андрю'с чурч, хексхам, нортумбирланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Comyn родилась в 1248 г. в местности бучань, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Alexander Comyn 6th Earl of Buchan, Constable and Justiciar of Scotland было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Quincy, 28. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angus. Она умерла 17 февраля 1328 г. в местности прудхо, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 80 лет и была похоронена в местности ст андрю'с чурч, хексхам, нортумбирланд, Англия.

Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir Gilbert de Umfraville 8th Earl of Angus. Murió el 17 de febrero de 1328, en Prudhoe, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultada en St Andrew's Church, Hexham, Northumberland, Inglaterra.
